title: Brain client: Changes(since) over GET /changes
userStory: As the resolver, I want a typed client call for the brain's Tier 0 change signal so I can cheaply ask 'has anything moved since this cursor?' without a recall fan-out or an LLM call.
acceptanceCriteria: internal/brainbot/client.go gains ChangesResult { Cursor string `json:"cursor"`; Changed bool `json:"changed"` } and Client.Changes(ctx context.Context, since string) (ChangesResult, error), following the existing getJSON(ctx, path, query, out) pattern used by Map()/Doc()
since is passed as the `since` query param; an empty since is sent as-is (the brain returns changed=true when since is absent/stale — do not special-case it client-side)

A doc comment states the contract verbatim from brainbot/docs/consumer-api.md: cursor is opaque (equality-compare only), the cursor is COARSER than /doc's version (a no-op re-sync advances it), so callers must gate any expensive recompute on their own basis, not on the cursor alone
Method is a no-op-safe read: it never writes; it errors (not panics) when the client is not Enabled(), consistent with newRequest()

[Verification] With the brain running locally (default http://127.0.0.1:8100), `curl 'http://127.0.0.1:8100/changes'` returns {cursor, changed:true}; calling Changes(ctx, thatCursor) returns changed:false. If the brain is not running, a unit test against an httptest stub returning the documented JSON shape proves decode + query-param wiring. Cite the stub/curl result in the completion note.
